calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Boessenecker for Ehlanie Palacpac (Petitioner).
Justin Goodwin for W.C. Cox & Co.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that time spent on the 850 Petition was addressed in his declaration, that there was an indication by the Court that it was not the appropriate mechanism, that he used it here in this Court about fifteen to twenty years ago, that he did the unlawful detainer and that he paid $2,000.00 to get the caregiver out of the property. Mr. Boessenecker also stated that he had to get death certificates for the Grandmother who died in 1979 and the Father who died in 2008, that they were never probated, that he had to go back and do multiple documents and parent transfer exclusions, that he defended a lawsuit for adverse possession of property for several years, that he brought a summary judgment motion, that he put a lot of time into it, and that there were four different lawsuits in this case. In addition, Mr. Boessenecker stated that he defended his Client individually and as Administrator, spoke of the Palou Street property and the preliminary distribution that was done, and stated that his Client wants the assignment paid. Mr. Goodwin stated prior to the Daughter being located there had been a fraudulent petition filed indicating that there were no children, that his Client does not keep hourly records or have an hourly breakdown, referred to the declaration he filed, spoke of work performed by his Client (going through microfilm/legwork) and stated that his Client did a reduction in the percentage of fees. The Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

Off Calendar Apr-24-2017 Continued To May-08-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Recovery Possession Of Real Property Belonging To Decendent
Off Calendar Nov-26-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Ehlanie Palacpac (Petitioner/Administrator). Richard Hurlburt for and with Maria Palacpac (Respondent).

The Court stated that this sounds like an unlawful detainer case. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to issue of the Probate Court having jurisdiction under 850 and why the matter should remain in Probate. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court stated that the proper way to proceed is by way of a complaint/unlawful detainer. The Petition to Recover Possession of Real Property Belonging to Decedent was denied without prejudice in favor of proceeding in an alternate mechanism (order for the same to be prepared by Mr. Boessenecker.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
